docs.title: funstat-api
docs.text:
Python client for the Funstat API — Telegram user and group statistics.

Supports both sync and async modes out of the box.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install funstat-api

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
SyncAsync 
from funstat_api import FunstatClient

with FunstatClient("your_token") as fs:
    stats = fs.stats("durov")
    print(stats.data.total_msg_count)

import asyncio
from funstat_api import AsyncFunstatClient

async def main():
    async with AsyncFunstatClient("your_token") as fs:
        stats = await fs.stats("durov")
        print(stats.data.total_msg_count)

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Sync client powered by requests
  • ✅ Async client powered by httpx
  • ✅ Fully typed — all responses are Pydantic models
  • ✅ Accepts username, @username, numeric ID, or t.me/ link
  • ✅ Auto-resolves usernames to IDs internally

Get Started

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• PyPI
  • GitHub
  • Funstat

docs.title: 0.1.0 — Initial release
docs.text:
• Sync client FunstatClient powered by requests
  • Async client AsyncFunstatClient powered by httpx
  • Full Pydantic v2 models for all API responses
  • Auto-resolution of usernames, @username, and t.me/ links
  • Context manager support for both clients

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • A Funstat API token — get one at funstat.info

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install funstat-api

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Pass your token when creating a client:

from funstat_api import FunstatClient

fs = FunstatClient("your_token_here")

docs.title: Checking the connection
docs.text:
ping = fs.ping()
print(ping.responce_ping)   # round-trip time in seconds

balance = fs.get_balance()
print(balance.current_ballance)

docs.title: Identifying users
docs.text:
All methods that accept a user argument are flexible — you can pass:

fs.stats(12345678)               # numeric ID
fs.stats("durov")                # username without @
fs.stats("@durov")               # username with @
fs.stats("https://t.me/durov")   # full t.me link

The same applies to group arguments.

docs.title: Context managers
docs.text:
Both clients support context managers to ensure connections are closed properly:

SyncAsync 
with FunstatClient("your_token") as fs:
    print(fs.stats("durov"))

async with AsyncFunstatClient("your_token") as fs:
    print(await fs.stats("durov"))

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
from funstat_api import FunstatClient, ResolveError, ApiError

fs = FunstatClient("your_token")

try:
    stats = fs.stats("nonexistent_user_xyz")
except ResolveError as e:
    print(f"User not found: {e}")
except ApiError as e:
    print(f"API error {e.status_code} on {e.path}")

docs.title: Async Client
docs.text: from funstat_api import AsyncFunstatClient

docs.title: AsyncFunstatClient(token)
docs.text:
Creates an asynchronous client. Uses httpx under the hood. All methods must be await-ed.

Parameter Type Description token str Your Funstat API token

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
All methods are identical to the Sync Client but are coroutines — prefix every call with await. Return types and parameters are the same — refer to Sync Client for the full reference and Models for response type details.

async with AsyncFunstatClient("your_token") as fs:
    stats    = await fs.stats("durov")
    messages = await fs.get_messages("durov", limit=50)
    members  = await fs.get_group_members("https://t.me/mychat")
    usage    = await fs.username_usage("durov")

docs.title: Parallel requests
docs.text:
Because the client is async, you can fire multiple requests concurrently with asyncio.gather:

import asyncio
from funstat_api import AsyncFunstatClient

async def main():
    async with AsyncFunstatClient("your_token") as fs:
        stats, chats, names = await asyncio.gather(
            fs.stats("durov"),       # → UserStatsResponse
            fs.get_chats("durov"),   # → UsrChatInfoResponse
            fs.get_names("durov"),   # → UserNameInfoResponse
        )

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: close
docs.text:
async close()

Closes the underlying httpx.AsyncClient. Called automatically when using async with.

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text: from funstat_api import FunstatError, ResolveError, ApiError

docs.title: FunstatError
docs.text:
Base exception for all funstat errors. Catch this to handle any library error:

from funstat_api import FunstatError

try:
    result = fs.stats("someone")
except FunstatError as e:
    print(f"Something went wrong: {e}")

docs.title: ResolveError
docs.text:
Raised when a username or group cannot be resolved to a numeric ID. See resolve_username and username_usage for context.

from funstat_api import ResolveError

try:
    result = fs.stats("nonexistent_xyz_404")
except ResolveError as e:
    print(f"Not found: {e}")

docs.title: ApiError
docs.text:
Raised when the API returns a non-200 HTTP status code.

Attribute Type Description status_code int HTTP status code returned by the API path str API path that was requested 
from funstat_api import ApiError

try:
    result = fs.stats("durov")
except ApiError as e:
    print(f"HTTP {e.status_code} on {e.path}")

docs.title: Exception hierarchy
docs.text:
FunstatError
├── ResolveError   # username/group not found
└── ApiError       # non-200 HTTP response

docs.title: Models
docs.text:
All API responses are typed Pydantic v2 models. Import them directly:

from funstat_api import UserStats, GroupMember, ChatInfo  # etc.

docs.title: Response wrappers
docs.text:
Every method returns a response wrapper with a consistent shape:

Field Type Description success bool Whether the request succeeded tech TechInfo Request cost and balance info data ... \| None The actual payload paging Paging\| None Pagination info (paginated endpoints only)

docs.title: TechInfo
docs.text:
class TechInfo:
    request_cost: float
    current_ballance: float
    request_duration: str

docs.title: Paging
docs.text:
class Paging:
    total: int
    current_page: int
    page_size: int
    total_pages: int

docs.title: ResolvedUser
docs.text:
class ResolvedUser:
    id: int
    username: str | None
    first_name: str | None
    last_name: str | None
    is_active: bool
    is_bot: bool
    has_premium: bool | None

docs.title: UserStatsMin
docs.text:
class UserStatsMin:
    id: int
    first_name: str | None
    last_name: str | None
    is_bot: bool
    is_active: bool
    first_msg_date: str | None
    last_msg_date: str | None
    total_msg_count: int
    msg_in_groups_count: int
    adm_in_groups: int
    usernames_count: int
    names_count: int
    total_groups: int

docs.title: UserStats
docs.text:
Extends UserStatsMin with additional fields:

class UserStats(UserStatsMin):
    is_cyrillic_primary: bool | None
    lang_code: str | None
    unique_percent: float | None
    circle_count: int
    voice_count: int
    reply_percent: float
    media_percent: float
    link_percent: float
    favorite_chat: ChatInfo | None
    media_usage: str | None
    stars_val: int | None
    gift_count: int | None
    about: str | None
    # ... and more

docs.title: ChatInfo
docs.text:
class ChatInfo:
    id: int
    title: str
    is_private: bool
    username: str | None

docs.title: ChatInfoExt
docs.text:
Extends ChatInfo:

class ChatInfoExt(ChatInfo):
    is_channel: bool
    link: str | None

docs.title: GroupMember
docs.text:
class GroupMember:
    id: int
    username: str | None
    name: str | None
    first_name: str | None
    last_name: str | None
    is_admin: bool | None
    is_active: bool
    today_msg: int
    has_prem: bool | None
    has_photo: bool
    dc_id: int | None

docs.title: UserMsg
docs.text:
class UserMsg:
    date: str
    message_id: int
    reply_to_message_id: int | None
    media_code: int | None
    media_name: str | None
    text: str | None
    group: ChatInfo

docs.title: UserNameInfo
docs.text:
class UserNameInfo:
    name: str
    date_time: str

docs.title: UsrChatInfo
docs.text:
class UsrChatInfo:
    chat: ChatInfo
    last_message_id: int
    messages_count: int
    last_message: str | None
    first_message: str | None
    is_admin: bool
    is_left: bool

docs.title: GiftRelationInfo
docs.text:
class GiftRelationInfo:
    last_gift_date: str | None
    from_user_id: int
    from_first_name: str | None
    from_last_name: str | None
    from_main_username: str | None
    from_is_active: bool
    to_user_id: int
    to_first_name: str | None
    to_last_name: str | None
    to_main_username: str | None
    to_is_active: bool

docs.title: StickerInfo
docs.text:
class StickerInfo:
    sticker_set_id: int
    last_seen: str
    min_seen: str
    resolved: str | None
    title: str | None
    short_name: str | None
    stickers_count: int | None

docs.title: UCommonGroupInfo
docs.text:
class UCommonGroupInfo:
    user_id: int
    common_groups: int
    first_name: str | None
    last_name: str | None
    username: str | None
    is_user_active: bool

docs.title: UsernameUsageModel
docs.text:
class UsernameUsageModel:
    actual_users: list[ResolvedUser] | None
    usage_by_users_in_the_past: list[ResolvedUser] | None
    actual_groups_or_channels: list[ChatInfoExt] | None
    mention_by_channel_or_group_desc: list[ChatInfoExt] | None

docs.title: WhoWroteText
docs.text:
class WhoWroteText:
    message_id: int
    user_id: int
    date: str
    name: str | None
    username: str | None
    is_active: bool
    group: ChatInfoExt
    text: str | None

docs.title: WhoWroteTextPaged
docs.text:
class WhoWroteTextPaged:
    total: int
    data: list[WhoWroteText]
    is_last_page: bool | None
    page_size: int | None
    current_page: int | None
    total_pages: int | None

docs.title: PingResult
docs.text:
class PingResult:
    request_ping: str     # duration reported by the API
    responce_ping: float  # actual round-trip in seconds

docs.title: Response wrappers reference
docs.text: Class .data type ResolvedUserResponse list[ResolvedUser] UserStatsMinResponse UserStatsMin UserStatsResponse UserStats UserMsgPagedResponse list[UserMsg] UserNameInfoResponse list[UserNameInfo] UsrChatInfoResponse list[UsrChatInfo] GroupMemberResponse list[GroupMember] GiftRelationResponse list[GiftRelationInfo] StickerInfoResponse list[StickerInfo] UCommonGroupInfoResponse list[UCommonGroupInfo] UsernameUsageResponse UsernameUsageModel ChatInfoExtResponse list[ChatInfoExt] WhoWroteTextResponse WhoWroteTextPaged

docs.title: ResolvedUserResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[ResolvedUser]

docs.title: UserStatsMinResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: UserStatsMin

docs.title: UserStatsResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: UserStats

docs.title: UserMsgPagedResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · Paging · data: list[UserMsg]

docs.title: UserNameInfoResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[UserNameInfo]

docs.title: UsrChatInfoResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[UsrChatInfo]

docs.title: GroupMemberResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[GroupMember]

docs.title: GiftRelationResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[GiftRelationInfo]

docs.title: StickerInfoResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[StickerInfo]

docs.title: UCommonGroupInfoResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[UCommonGroupInfo]

docs.title: UsernameUsageResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: UsernameUsageModel

docs.title: ChatInfoExtResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: list[ChatInfoExt]

docs.title: WhoWroteTextResponse
docs.text: success · TechInfo · data: WhoWroteTextPaged

docs.title: Sync Client
docs.text: from funstat_api import FunstatClient

docs.title: FunstatClient(token)
docs.text:
Creates a synchronous client. Uses requests under the hood.

Parameter Type Description token str Your Funstat API token

docs.title: ping
docs.text:
ping() → PingResult | None

Check API availability and measure round-trip latency.

result = fs.ping()
print(result.responce_ping)   # seconds

docs.title: get_balance
docs.text:
get_balance() → TechInfo | None

Get current token balance and cost info.

balance = fs.get_balance()
print(balance.current_ballance)

docs.title: resolve_username
docs.text:
resolve_username(username) → ResolvedUserResponse | None

Resolve a username to full user info.

result = fs.resolve_username("durov")
user = result.data[0]
print(user.id, user.first_name)

docs.title: basic_info_by_id
docs.text:
basic_info_by_id(ids) → ResolvedUserResponse | None

Get basic info for one or multiple users by numeric ID.

result = fs.basic_info_by_id([123, 456])

docs.title: stats_min
docs.text:
stats_min(user) → UserStatsMinResponse | None

Get minimal statistics for a user.

result = fs.stats_min("durov")
print(result.data.total_msg_count)

docs.title: stats
docs.text:
stats(user) → UserStatsResponse | None

Get full statistics for a user.

result = fs.stats("durov")
print(result.data.reply_percent)

docs.title: messages_count
docs.text:
messages_count(user) → int

Get total message count for a user.

docs.title: groups_count
docs.text:
groups_count(user, only_msg=False) → int

Get number of groups a user belongs to. Set only_msg=True to count only groups where the user has sent messages.

docs.title: get_messages
docs.text:
get_messages(user, filter=None, group=None, limit=20, page=1) → UserMsgPagedResponse | None

Get paginated message history for a user.

Parameter Type Description user int \| str User identifier filter str \| None Filter by text content group int \| str \| None Filter by group limit int Page size (default 20) page int Page number (default 1) 
result = fs.get_messages("durov", filter="hello", limit=50)
for msg in result.data:
    print(msg.date, msg.text)

docs.title: get_chats
docs.text:
get_chats(user) → UsrChatInfoResponse | None

Get list of chats the user participates in.

docs.title: get_names
docs.text:
get_names(user) → UserNameInfoResponse | None

Get name change history for a user.

docs.title: get_usernames
docs.text:
get_usernames(user) → UserNameInfoResponse | None

Get username change history for a user.

docs.title: get_stickers
docs.text:
get_stickers(user) → StickerInfoResponse | None

Get sticker packs used by a user.

docs.title: get_gifts
docs.text:
get_gifts(user, limit=20, page=1) → GiftRelationResponse | None

Get gift relations (sent/received) for a user.

docs.title: common_groups
docs.text:
common_groups(user) → UCommonGroupInfoResponse | None

Get statistics on common groups with other users.

docs.title: rep
docs.text:
rep(user) → dict | None

Get reputation data for a user.

docs.title: username_usage
docs.text:
username_usage(username) → UsernameUsageResponse | None

Find who currently uses or has previously used a username.

result = fs.username_usage("durov")
print(result.data.actual_users)

docs.title: common_groups_for_users
docs.text:
common_groups_for_users(ids) → ChatInfoExtResponse | None

Find groups that a list of users all share.

result = fs.common_groups_for_users([123, 456, 789])

docs.title: get_group_info
docs.text:
get_group_info(group) → dict | None

Get raw info about a group or channel.

docs.title: get_group_members
docs.text:
get_group_members(group) → GroupMemberResponse | None

Get member list of a group.

result = fs.get_group_members("https://t.me/mychat")
for member in result.data:
    print(member.id, member.username)

docs.title: search_text
docs.text:
search_text(query, page=1, page_size=20) → WhoWroteTextResponse | None

Search all indexed messages by text content.

result = fs.search_text("hello world")
for item in result.data.data:
    print(item.user_id, item.text)

docs.title: close
docs.text:
close()

Close the underlying HTTP session. Called automatically when using with.
